improvements_list: Constitution glossary trimmed from ~65 entries to ~20 true declarations, eliminating near-duplication with Appendix A (INV-015 compliance)
Quality gates reformatted from prose to tabular declaration form in constitution, reducing Tier 1 overhead while preserving full definitions in owning modules
Performance budgets condensed to declaration form in constitution with pointer to core-standard full definitions
Three new negative specifications added to element-specifications Chapter 4: monolithic formal model block, ASCII-only state machines, and prose guard conditions
Authoring sequence steps labeled with explicit identifiers (GOAL, FORMAL, NON-NEG, INV, ADR, IMPL, etc.) making the dependency DAG machine-navigable
Module header format documented with both YAML frontmatter (preferred) and comment-style alternatives with full examples
Meta-standard carve-out added to INV-012 explaining how Section Map mediates cross-module references in two-tier mode
Self-bootstrapping note added to §5.1 explaining how meta-standards adapt the 13-component requirement
Two new risk register entries addressing modular-specific risks: declaration-definition drift and assembly tooling unavailability
New Master TODO section I tracking modular self-conformance items with 6 specific checkable items
LLM term added to Appendix A glossary with DDIS-specific context definition referencing §0.2.2
Verification prompt added to modularization module for §0.13
Element-specifications module header clarified with inline comment explaining why maintains is empty
Constitution section numbering improved with §0.5 and §0.6 prefixes for navigability
invariant_violations: INV-016: Section Map accuracy not validated after modular restructuring edits (acknowledged in Master TODO I as unchecked item)
INV-016: Context budget values in constitution not verified against manifest.yaml (acknowledged in Master TODO I as unchecked item)
gate_failures: Gate 6: Implementation Readiness not externally validated — no non-trivial domain spec written conforming to DDIS (acknowledged in Master TODO H)
Gate 7: LLM Implementation Readiness not tested — no LLM given spec chapters to implement (acknowledged in Master TODO H)
Gate M-3: LLM Bundle Sufficiency not tested — no bundles given to LLM for isolated implementation (acknowledged in Master TODO G)
Gate M-4: Declaration-Definition Faithfulness not systematically verified — constitution declarations vs module definitions need formal comparison (acknowledged in Master TODO I)
remaining_gaps: External validation infrastructure: Gates 6, 7, and M-3 all require giving spec content to an actual LLM or human implementer — this is the largest remaining gap and blocks the 'Validated' lifecycle state
Assembly and validation tooling not built: ddis_assemble.sh and ddis_validate.sh referenced throughout but not yet implemented — blocks mechanical Gate M-1 checking

Cross-module reference audit incomplete: the meta-standard carve-out for INV-012 is well-reasoned but the actual modules need systematic audit to confirm all cross-module references use the Section Map-mediated pattern
Section Map accuracy after edits: modular restructuring may have introduced section range drift in the Section Map table — needs mechanical verification
Declaration-definition faithfulness: the trimmed constitution glossary and condensed gate/budget declarations need formal comparison against module definitions for INV-015 and Gate M-4
No domain-spec worked example: the spec prescribes a format but has never been applied to a non-meta-standard domain spec — this limits confidence in INV-008 for real-world use
Proportional weight self-analysis: the modular form's weight distribution should be analyzed against the meta-standard proportional weight targets in §0.8.2 to verify self-conformance
Manifest.yaml synchronization: the constitution references manifest values but the manifest file itself was not part of the versioned snapshot for verification
rationale: v1 delivers 15 substantive improvements over the seed with zero regressions, achieving strong self-bootstrapping adherence across all 20 invariants and 11 ADRs. The most impactful changes — trimming the constitution glossary to true declarations, condensing gates/budgets to declaration form, and adding the INV-012 meta-standard carve-out — materially improve both modular self-conformance (INV-015) and LLM context efficiency. However, the score remains below 90 primarily because external validation (Gates 6, 7, M-3) has never been performed, assembly/validation tooling is unbuilt, and several modular self-conformance checks (Section Map accuracy, declaration-definition faithfulness, cross-module reference audit) remain unvalidated — all of which are clearly addressable in subsequent iterations. The next iteration should prioritize: (1) systematic INV-012 cross-module reference audit, (2) INV-015/Gate M-4 declaration-definition comparison, (3) INV-016 Section Map and context budget verification, and (4) proportional weight self-analysis.
